LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JOSEPH BRADFORD'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; NOT REPORTED.
NOTICE OF MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF RYS, CHRISTOPHER ,AN INDIVIDUAL HEARING SET FOR JUL-10-2012 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, NEITHER PARTY CONTESTED THE TENTATIVE RULING. THEREFORE, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF CHRISTOPHER RYS' MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION IS GRANTED. THE COURT VACATES THE ORDERS FILED ON JUNE 1, 2012 SUSTAINING THE DEMURRER AS TO THE FIRST, THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION AND GRANTING THE MOTION TO STRIKE. UPON RECONSIDERATION OF THE DEMURRER AND MOTION TO STRIKE, THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION AND SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION AND THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED AS TO PARAGRAPH 16 AND THE PRAYER AND OFF CALENDAR AS TO PARAGRAPH 19. ASSUMING WITHOUT DECIDING THAT THE FIRST AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION DO NOT RELATE BACK TO THE ORIGINAL COMPLAINT FOR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS PURPOSES, NONETHELESS THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT DISCLOSE ON ITS FACE THAT THE FIRST AND THIRD CLAIMS ARE NECESSARILY TIME-BARRED. NOR ARE THOSE CLAIMS UNCERTAIN. THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS BECAUSE THE TORT OF STRICT LIABILITY IS INAPPLICABLE TO THE FACTS ALLEGED, NOR IS IT LIKELY THAT THIS DEFICIENCY CAN BE CURED BY AN AMENDMENT. THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS ARE SUFFICIENT BECAUSE LIBERALLY CONSTRUED THE THIRD CLAIM ALLEGES LIABILITY FOR PROMISSORY FRAUD FOR WHICH PUNITIVE DAMAGES ARE AVAILABLE, NOT BAD FAITH BREACH OF CONTRACT AS TO WHICH PUNITIVE DAMAGES ARE NOT AVAILABLE. IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON THE DEMURRER TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION, THE MOTION TO STRIKE PARAGRAPH 19 IS MOOT. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR #5976

Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration
Off Calendar Jul-20-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Christopher Rys, Tel. 415.269.1460, plaintiff and moving party appearing on his own behalf;
Matthew R. Halloran, Esq. of Bledsoe Cathcart Diestel Pedersen & Treppa LLP, Tel. 415.981.5411, appearing for Defendant and opposing party.

Neither party contested the tentative ruling. Therefore, the court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF CHRISTOPHER RYS' Motion For Reconsideration is Granted. The court vacates the orders filed on June 1, 2012 sustaining the demurrer as to the first, third and fourth causes of action and granting the motion to strike. Upon reconsideration of the demurrer and motion to strike, the demurrer is overruled as to the first and third causes of action and sustained without leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action and the motion to strike is denied as to paragraph 16 and the prayer and off calendar as to paragraph 19. Assuming without deciding that the first and third causes of action do not relate back to the original complaint for statute of limitations purposes, nonetheless the complaint does not disclose on its face that the first and third claims are necessarily time-barred. Nor are those claims uncertain. The fourth cause of action fails because the tort of strict liability is inapplicable to the facts alleged, nor is it likely that this deficiency can be cured by an amendment. The punitive damages allegations are sufficient because liberally construed the third claim alleges liability for promissory fraud for which punitive damages are available, not bad faith breach of contract as to which punitive damages are not available. In light of the ruling on the demurrer to the fourth cause of action, the motion to strike paragraph 19 is moot. Defendant's counsel to prepare a form of order. Judge: Harold Kahn; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR #5976

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF CHRISTOPHER RYS' Motion For Leave To File Amended Complaint IS Granted and the trial is continued to December 3, 2012 at 9:30am in department 206 with all discovery and summary motion dates triggered off the new trial date. The strong policy of liberally granting amendments and any prejudice to defendants eliminated by the short trial continuance mandates the granting of the motion. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.
